news.title: Goa consumer commission orders Dimension Builders to form flat owners' society
news.para: A builder must form a society for flat owners in Dimension Residency within three months. If the builder fails, residents can form it themselves. This order comes after a flat owner complained about defects. The commission noted the builder's failure to address issues. Compensation was denied due to the complaint being filed late.

news.title: Bombay HC quashes DRT's stay order on post-sale process of mortgaged flat in Mumbai auctioned by bank
news.para: The Bombay High Court has overturned a Debt Recovery Tribunal order that halted the sale of a south Mumbai flat. The court noted a concerning trend where borrowers delay action until auctions are finalized. They then use insolvency proceedings to stall possession. This ruling provides relief to auction purchasers.

news.title: Global investors eye GIFT City amid West Asia conflict
news.para: Rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia are prompting global investors and NRIs to reassess their Dubai exposure, leading to increased inquiries for India's GIFT City. Investors are seeking regulatory stability and proximity to growth markets, driving a 'dual-hub' strategy rather than a complete relocation from Dubai.

news.title: Embassy REIT targets 13 million sq ft acquisitions to scale income
news.para: Embassy Office Parks REIT is evaluating a 12-13 million sq ft pipeline, including 4 million sq ft from its sponsor, to scale its portfolio and enhance income visibility. The REIT has raised Rs 9,800 crore in FY26 and has a Rs 4,000 crore development pipeline, aiming for yield-accretive Grade A assets.

news.title: Telangana government amends building rules, expands TDR use
news.para: Telangana government has amended building rules, streamlining TDR usage and relaxing parameters to boost real estate. High-rise definitions are updated, and TDR can now be used for setback relaxations and to offset development charges for altered master plan roads. These changes aim to provide greater flexibility and financial ease for developers.

news.title: ED flags Indians buying Dubai properties using credit cards
news.para: Indian authorities are scrutinizing residents who bought Dubai properties using credit cards. This method may breach foreign exchange rules. Individuals are receiving notices from the Enforcement Directorate. They face penalties and the need to regularize transactions. Some may have to sell properties. This situation highlights the complexities of cross-border property investments.

news.title: Manufacturing push drives property developers to build industrial townships
news.para: Property developers are increasingly focusing on integrated industrial townships to support India's manufacturing growth ambitions. This strategic shift aims to create cohesive ecosystems with housing and social infrastructure alongside industrial land, positioning industrial real estate as a stable investment avenue.

news.title: Hyderabad's 'far west' is booming, but is the growth sustainable?
news.para: Hyderabad's western outskirts are experiencing a rapid surge in housing projects, particularly in areas like Kollur and Tellapur, with prices doubling in five years. Despite tempting offers, these 'budget housing' hubs often lack basic amenities, raising concerns about oversupply and potential project stagnation.

news.title: NCLT allows withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against ATS Knightsbridge
news.para: NCLT has permitted the withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against Noida's ATS Knightsbridge residential project after a settlement with lenders. The Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process, initiated in 2023, was formally closed on March 13, 2026. The project is nearing completion, with a tentative handover expected in 18-24 months.

news.title: About 7.6 lakh streetlights in Hyderabad set for ₹1,340-crore overhaul
news.para: Greater Hyderabad is set for a major street lighting overhaul with over 70% of existing lights nearing end-of-life. A Rs 1,340 crore, 10-year unified tender will replace, operate, and maintain approximately 7.6 lakh streetlights and CCMS boxes across three municipal corporations. The new system emphasizes real-time monitoring, performance-based penalties, and faster fault rectification to ensure improved illumination and accountability.

news.title: Vedanta moves NCLAT, challenges NCLT nod to Adani's bid for acquiring Jaiprakash Associates
news.para: Vedanta Group has appealed to the NCLAT against the NCLT's decision. The NCLT had approved Adani Group's bid to acquire Jaiprakash Associates Ltd. Vedanta was also in the race to acquire the company. The Committee of Creditors had previously approved Adani's plan. Jaiprakash Associates has significant assets across real estate, cement, and hospitality.

news.title: Chandigarh housing board to hike restoration charges for resumed properties
news.para: Chandigarh Housing Board is set to significantly hike restoration charges for properties resumed due to dues or violations, potentially by ₹1 lakh to ₹3 lakh. This move aims to curb unauthorized construction and encourage timely payments. Meanwhile, the administration seeks approval to regularize GPA property transfers, expecting to recover substantial lost revenue from stamp duty.

news.title: Over 350 government offices haven't paid Nagpur civic body ₹145 crore in property tax
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation is struggling to collect over 145 crore rupees in property tax from government departments. This is a major hurdle as the civic body aims to meet its revenue targets. Despite efforts and issuing notices, recovery from these departments remains slow. The corporation is considering a property tax hike for citizens to compensate for the shortfall.

news.title: Pune: PMC to take legal action after 4,000 cheque bounce cases recorded for property tax payment
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation is taking strict legal action against property owners whose tax payment cheques have bounced. Over 4,200 cases were recorded this financial year. Around 3,600 owners have cleared dues, but legal proceedings will start for the remaining 600. The PMC aims to recover pending taxes before the March 31 deadline.

news.title: Telangana unveils Bharat future city plan to house up to 30 lakh residents
news.para: Telangana's ambitious "future city" project, spanning 30,000 acres, is set to house 25-30 lakh people. A master plan proposes a central business district with specialized zones for AI, health, education, and life sciences. The city aims to be a model of sustainable development, featuring advanced infrastructure and a focus on work, live, learn, and play principles.

news.title: Goa PCB issues show-cause notice to housing project in Vasco for discharging untreated sewage
news.para: Goa State Pollution Control Board has issued a show-cause notice to Prabhu Violetta Phase I residential complex in Vasco for discharging untreated sewage. The complex faces potential environmental compensation of Rs 44,50,000 and Rs 12,50,000 for violating pollution norms and operating without valid consent. The board ordered immediate cessation of discharge and daily sewage transport to a public facility.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: BMC issues stop-construction order to builder after residents' complaints
news.para: Bhubaneswar Municipal Corporation has halted construction at a Nandan Vihar housing project following resident complaints of unauthorized expansion and ORERA norm violations. Despite the builder's application rejection, work continued. Residents cited issues like unaddressed infrastructure and parking, prompting BMC's intervention and calls for stricter action against the developer.

news.title: Telangana high court stays acquisition of four Jubilee Hills properties
news.para: The Telangana High Court has halted the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation's acquisition of private land in Jubilee Hills. The GHMC planned a new road from Road No. 45 T-Junction to Durgam Cheruvu. The court found procedural flaws in the acquisition notice and publication.

news.title: Nashik: Revenue department issues revision notices to 439 people over alleged sub-division of their plots
news.para: Nashik authorities have issued revision notices to 439 individuals linked to 49 land deals, alleging fabricated sub-division maps were used to circumvent housing scheme regulations. Developers are accused of creating artificial land fragments under 4,000sqm to avoid reserving 20% for EWS/LIG housing, potentially costing Nashik 6,500 homes.

news.title: Jaipur development body expansion of no benefit to residential areas on outskirts
news.para: Residents in Jaipur's peripheral colonies face a struggle for basic civic amenities, with a recent sewerage tank issue highlighting the problem. The proposed expansion of the Jaipur Development Authority's jurisdiction under Master Plan 2047 is expected to worsen these difficulties by encouraging low-density growth in remote areas lacking adequate infrastructure.
